mechanism_narrative: The Federal Reserve has raised FEDFUNDS from 2.33% to an elevated level in a tightening cycle. Sustained elevated FEDFUNDS signals continued monetary restriction, which increases real discount rates and pushes 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market reprices long-term borrowing costs higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.33% as of August 2022 reflects early-stage tightening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS remaining elevated, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 3.7% signals a tight labor market with wage pressure. If UNRATE remains below 4.0%, sustained labor tightness drives inflation expectations higher, forcing the Fed to maintain or increase FEDFUNDS, which pushes 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market prices in longer-duration monetary restriction.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% indicates tight labor market conditions
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls over 20 trading days when UNRATE < 4.0% and FEDFUNDS is elevated, or the interaction coefficient between UNRATE and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 683,291 million dollars reflect strong consumer demand. Sustained high RSAFS signals robust aggregate demand that keeps inflation elevated, requiring the Fed to maintain restrictive policy. This demand-driven inflation expectation pushes 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 683,291M indicates strong consumer spending
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20 trading days despite RSAFS remaining elevated, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 104.55 (2017=100) reflects supply-side constraints relative to demand. If INDPRO growth stalls or declines while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, the market interprets this as stagflationary pressure, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as investors demand higher real yields to compensate for persistent inflation and weak growth.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.55 and CPIAUCSL at 295.62 suggest supply constraints with elevated inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls over 20 trading days when stagflationary conditions are present, or the interaction term between INDPRO weakness and CPIAUCSL elevation is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,446 thousand units signal residential demand. A decline in HOUST below 1,400 units over the next month indicates weakening demand from Fed tightening, which reduces growth expectations and causes 10-year Treasury yields to fall as the market reprices lower long-term growth and inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,446 thousand is near threshold for demand weakness signal
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises over 20 trading days when HOUST falls below 1,400, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 152,744 thousand reflects labor market strength. If PAYEMS growth decelerates to below 200 thousand new jobs per month, this signals weakening labor demand and recession risk, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall sharply as investors flee to safety and growth expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 200 thousand for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152,744 thousand reflects strong employment but vulnerable to deceleration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises over 60 trading days when PAYEMS growth falls below 200k, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 295.62 is elevated. If CPIAUCSL growth decelerates month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains above 2.5%, the market interprets this as successful disinflation, reducing long-term inflation expectations and causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as real yields compress.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 295.62 with FEDFUNDS at 2.33% reflects early tightening phase
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises over 20 trading days when disinflation signals are present, or the interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL deceleration and FEDFUNDS elevation is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 19,699.465 billion (2017 dollars) reflects aggregate demand. If GDPC1 growth turns negative in the next quarter (indicating recession), 10-year Treasury yields will fall sharply as investors reprice growth expectations downward and demand safe-haven assets, compressing long-term yields.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,699.465B as of Q2 2022 reflects pre-recession conditions
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises over 60 trading days when GDPC1 turns negative, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window during recession periods.
